newsSince: Highly material AI-compute escalation. At its June 24 Investor Day (one day after this eval), Qualcomm detailed an annual-cadence AI200/AI250 data-center inference roadmap, updated a 200MW accelerator agreement ramping in H2 2026, and announced NVLink Fusion-compatible data-center CPUs. The ByteDance ASIC deal (millions of units) and reported ~$10B Tenstorrent acquisition talks further raise data-center directness.

sources.label: Qualcomm Investor Day to detail data-center plans (Yahoo Finance)
Qualcomm ByteDance ASIC deal / sector unwind (24/7 Wall St)

verdicts.rankRationale: On the AI-node bottleneck lens, new evidence materially raises directness: Investor-Day AI200/AI250 rack-scale inference roadmap, a 200MW accelerator deal ramping H2'26, ByteDance ASICs, NVLink-Fusion CPUs and reported Tenstorrent talks move QCOM well beyond 'selective edge.' Re-rate up.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: AI200/AI250 inference roadmap + 200MW deal + ByteDance ASICs
verdicts.primaryRisk: Execution/ramp risk; export-control exposure on China demand
verdicts.bull: Credible rack-scale inference roadmap, hyperscale ASIC wins and possible Tenstorrent deal turn QCOM into a direct AI-compute bottleneck name.
verdicts.neutral: Strong narrative but data-center revenue still early; Investor-Day targets need 2026-27 proof points.
verdicts.bear: Inference accelerators face Nvidia/AMD/Broadcom; ByteDance reliance carries export-control and concentration risk.

verdicts.rankRationale: Through the co-packaged-optics lens, Qualcomm's data-center push (AI200/AI250, CPUs) strengthens AI-edge/inference optionality but it is still not a CPO/optical-cable play. Slightly less negative than the prior 'remove,' yet near the bottom of an actionable CPO ranking is appropriate.
verdicts.primaryRisk: No direct CPO/optical leverage; handset/China cycle
verdicts.bull: Expanding data-center silicon adds AI optionality that could one day attach to optical-interconnect ecosystems.
verdicts.neutral: Real AI momentum but irrelevant to the CPO/co-packaged-optics thesis this page screens for.
verdicts.bear: No co-packaged-optics content; handset cyclicality and China exposure dominate the standalone case here.
